decks.notes: This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and quick removal spells to overpower opponents early in the game. With a mix of low-cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, as well as efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm, the deck aims to maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to provide additional versatility and win conditions. By combining speed and disruption, this deck aims to quickly close out games against slower opponents.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand using Goryo's Vengeance, while also utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Psychic Frog for control and card advantage. It includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo. The deck also has access to a variety of lands for mana fixing and consistency. Overall, the strategy revolves around quickly putting pressure on opponents with big threats and controlling the game with efficient instant spells.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones, and then bring them back onto the battlefield using spells like Persist and Unearth. This combo-reanimator archetype focuses on controlling the board with disruption spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize while setting up big plays to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Faithless Looting and Thought Scour to dig through the deck and assemble the necessary pieces for a successful reanimation strategy.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana using Birthing Ritual and efficient creatures like Coiling Oracle and Ice-Fang Coatl to overwhelm the opponent with aggression. The deck includes disruptive spells like Flare of Denial and Sink into Stupor to control the board and protect against threats. The combination of efficient creatures, mana acceleration, and disruption allows the deck to apply pressure early on while also being able to adapt to different situations and strategies from the opponent.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and efficiently deal damage to the opponent using cheap and aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck also focuses on card selection and manipulation to maintain a fast and consistent pace, with cards like Expressive Iteration and Preordain. Additionally, cards like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge provide additional ways to boost the damage output of the creatures in the deck, ensuring a relentless assault on the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly playing artifacts and using them to power up creatures while disrupting opponents with cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke. It aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggressive creatures like Arcbound Ravager and efficient removal like Galvanic Blast. The key strategy is to generate early board presence and maintain pressure with a combination of powerful artifacts and creatures. The deck also utilizes cards like Tormod's Crypt and Pithing Needle to disrupt opponent strategies while maintaining a consistent flow of artifacts with cards like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal.

This deck focuses on using the UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp up mana, enabling the player to cast powerful colorless Eldrazi creatures and control spells. The key strategy is to accelerate mana production with UrzaTron lands, search for key pieces with Expedition Map, and disrupt opponents' plans with control spells like Trinisphere and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. The deck's win condition revolves around casting massive Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger to overwhelm the opponent.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to maintain control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by countering their spells, removing their threats, and controlling the board with powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. The deck also includes cards like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive for card selection and card advantage. Overall, the deck is designed to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

This deck focuses on using efficient creatures and spells to apply pressure on the opponent quickly, aiming to end the game before the opponent can stabilize. The deck utilizes a mix of aggressive creatures like Kroxa, Ragavan, and Territorial Kavu, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Lightning Bolt to clear the way for attacks. Leyline cards provide additional support by boosting creature stats and providing protection. The mana base is designed to support the deck's multicolored spells, allowing for consistency in casting threats and disruption.

This Boros Ponza deck aims to control the game by using disruptive spells like Cleansing Wildfire, Path to Exile, and Wrath of God to remove threats from the opponent's board. It also utilizes cards like Galvanic Discharge and Solitude for additional removal and protection. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also ramping up into powerful threats like Chandra, Torch of Defiance and The Legend of Roku. By disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Field of Ruin and Demolition Field, the deck aims to gain a strategic advantage and come out on top in the late game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Broodscale Bloodchief combo using cards like Basking Broodscale and Blade of the Bloodchief. The deck utilizes various Eldrazi creatures and support cards to control the board and ramp up to play Emrakul, the Promised End. With the help of cards like Malevolent Rumble and Unholy Heat, the deck can deal with threats efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Ancient Stirrings and Urza's Saga help in finding key pieces of the combo quickly, ensuring a fast and powerful win condition.

This deck focuses on filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones, then using cards like Persist and Unearth to bring them back onto the battlefield quickly. Faithless Looting, Thought Scour, and Psychic Frog help to fill the graveyard while also providing card advantage. With a combination of control cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, along with disruptive spells like Spell Pierce, this deck aims to reanimate its threats and overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures early in the game.

This Ruby Storm deck focuses on combining fast mana generation with card draw and spell recursion to quickly build up storm count and ultimately win the game with a large Grapeshot or Ral, Monsoon Mage damage. By using cards like Manamorphose, Desperate Ritual, and Pyretic Ritual to generate mana, along with spells like Reckless Impulse and Wish to draw cards, the deck aims to chain together spells and create a high storm count for a lethal finish. Additionally, Past in Flames allows for a secondary win condition by casting previously used spells from the graveyard.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly playing low-cost creatures and spells to deal damage to the opponent as efficiently as possible. Utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler to pressure the opponent early on, combined with cheap burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck aims to maintain board presence and chip away at the opponent's life total quickly. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation with cards like Preordain and Mishra's Bauble to ensure consistent card advantage and keep up with the aggression.

The deck focuses on abusing the powerful UrzaTron lands to quickly produce a large amount of mana, allowing for the casting of expensive and impactful spells. It aims to control the game through disruption and removal spells while also deploying threats like Eldrazi creatures to close out the game. Karn, the Great Creator is a key card that can shut down opposing strategies, while Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and All Is Dust are used to clear the board if needed. The deck aims to maintain control of the game while presenting threats that are difficult for opponents to handle.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing powerful cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Ugin, the Spirit Dragon, and All Is Dust to dominate the board. It aims to assemble the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to generate massive amounts of mana to cast big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck also utilizes Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to disrupt opponents' strategies and uses artifacts like Vexing Bauble and Expedition Map for card advantage and consistency.
